steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Use the table points $$(-4,0)$$ and $$(-\frac{19}{5},1)$$ to find the slope of the linear function.
$$m=\frac{1-0}{-\frac{19}{5}-(-4)}=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{5}}=5$$

steps.title: Find the starting function
steps.content:
Substitute $(-4,0)$ into $f(x)=5x+b$.
$$0=5(-4)+b$$
$$b=20$$
So the function from the table is $f(x)=5x+20$.

steps.title: Apply the shift
steps.content:
Since $h(x)=f(x)-13$, subtract $13$ from the constant term of $f$.
$$h(x)=(5x+20)-13=5x+7$$
The answer is B.
